description: Chapter 2 - Construction of quantum theory I: wave mechanics. A guided auxiliary structure for the Schrodinger equation, wave functions, postulates, observables, expectation values, wave packets and uncertainty.

topics.title: Why wave mechanics is needed
topics.note: Chapter map: how de Broglie's matter waves become a dynamical theory for quantum states.

topics.title: Time-dependent Schrodinger equation
topics.note: From the plane wave, de Broglie's relation and the classical energy balance to the TDSE.

topics.title: Stationary states and the TISE
topics.note: Separation of variables, time-independent potentials and the energy eigenvalue equation.

topics.title: Probability density and current
topics.note: Born interpretation, continuity equation, normalization and probability in a spatial interval.

topics.title: Infinite potential well
topics.note: Boundary conditions, allowed sine functions, normalized eigenstates, nodes, probability densities and the quadratic energy spectrum.

topics.title: Postulates of quantum mechanics: the rules ahead
topics.note: Bridge card: after solving one model, the chapter states the rules for states, observables, probabilities and measurement.

topics.title: Quantum states as superpositions
topics.note: Postulate for quantum states: a physical state is represented by a wave function and can be expanded in an orthonormal basis.

topics.title: Observables, operators and commutators
topics.note: Postulate for observables: each measurable quantity is represented by a Hermitian operator; commutators encode compatibility and uncertainty.

topics.title: Measurement probabilities
topics.note: Postulate of probability: expansion coefficients give outcome probabilities, P_n = |C_n|^2, when measuring in an eigenstate basis.

topics.title: Expectation values and collapse
topics.note: Measurement postulate: expectation values are ensemble averages, and after a definite result the state collapses to the measured eigenstate.

topics.title: Equations of motion: Ehrenfest and Virial
topics.note: Time evolution of expectation values, the classical limit and the Virial theorem.

topics.title: Plane waves and localized packets
topics.note: Why a single plane wave has sharp momentum but poor localization, and why packets are needed.

topics.title: Gaussian wave packet and Fourier width
topics.note: Fourier transform, Gaussian distributions in x and k, and the inverse relation between widths.

topics.title: Wave packet spreading and uncertainty
topics.note: Group velocity, phase velocity, time-dependent variance and the Heisenberg uncertainty principle.

topics.title: Chapter synthesis
topics.note: Conceptual bridge from wave mechanics to the more abstract formulation of quantum theory.
